pv_basic.title: 常用動詞片語
pv_basic.summary: 動詞片語(phrasal verbs)是「動詞＋介系詞／副詞」的固定組合，意思常常無法從字面直接猜出，例如 give up 不是「給上去」而是「放棄」。這類片語在會考、學測的閱讀與克漏字中出現頻率極高，也是日常口說的核心。建議整組記憶，並特別留意固定搭配的介系詞(如 look forward to 後面接動名詞)，多用例句熟悉語感。

sections.body: 本單元收錄 47 個常用動詞片語。下面列出部分，完整清單請到「片語卡」翻閱。
examples: look after — 照顧、照料
give up — 放棄
put off — 拖延、延期
run out of — 用完、耗盡
get along with — 與(某人)相處融洽
look forward to — 期待、盼望
take care of — 照顧、處理
turn on — 打開(電器、開關)
turn off — 關掉(電器、開關)
pick up — 接送、撿起、學會
find out — 查明、發現(真相、資訊)
look for — 尋找
look up — 查閱(字典、資料)
look into — 調查、研究
grow up — 長大、成長
get up — 起床、起身
wake up — 醒來、叫醒
put on — 穿上、戴上

pv_adv.title: 進階動詞片語
pv_adv.summary: 進階動詞片語多由「動詞＋介系詞／副詞」組成，整體意思往往無法從字面直接猜出(例如 put up with 是「忍受」而非「舉起」)。這類片語是學測、會考閱讀與寫作的高頻重點，掌握它們能讓表達更道地。學習時建議連同搭配的受詞與情境一起記，並注意可分/不可分及後接名詞或 V-ing 的用法。

give in — 讓步、屈服
run into — 偶然遇見；遭遇(困難)

bring up — 養育；提出(話題)
point out — 指出

be_adj_prep.title: 形容詞+介系詞
be_adj_prep.summary: 「形容詞+介系詞」是英文中極常見的固定搭配，形容詞後面該接哪個介系詞往往沒有規則可循，必須整組記憶，例如 be good at(擅長)、be afraid of(害怕)。這類片語常以「be 動詞+形容詞+介系詞+名詞/動名詞」的形式出現，介系詞後面若接動詞要用動名詞(V-ing)。會考與學測的克漏字、詞彙題經常鎖定介系詞的正確搭配，建議成組背誦、搭配例句加深印象。

be capable of — 有能力做……
be responsible for — 對……負責
be fond of — 喜愛、喜歡
be tired of — 厭倦、受夠了
be full of — 充滿……
be similar to — 與……相似
be different from — 與……不同

be angry with — 對(某人)生氣

be sorry for — 為……感到抱歉、同情
be good to — 對(某人)友善、好

prep_phrase.title: 介系詞與名詞片語
prep_phrase.summary: 介系詞片語與名詞片語常以固定搭配出現，介系詞(in / on / at / by / for)後面接特定名詞，整組意思往往無法逐字翻譯，必須整組記憶。這類片語在會考、學測的克漏字與閱讀中出現頻率極高，熟記固定搭配能大幅減少介系詞選錯的失分。學習時建議連同例句一起背，掌握語境用法。

connector.title: 連接與轉折片語
connector.summary: 連接與轉折片語是文章的「路標」，幫助讀者掌握句與句、段與段之間的邏輯關係，例如因果、對比、舉例、補充與總結。在會考與學測的閱讀測驗與克漏字中，這類片語常是解題關鍵；寫作時善用它們也能讓文章更通順有條理。學習時建議依「功能」分類記憶(因果、轉折、舉例、補充、結論)，並注意 due to、because of 後接名詞，而 so that 後接子句等文法差異。

time_freq.title: 時間頻率與程度片語
time_freq.summary: 這一類片語用來表達「時間、頻率與程度」：有的說明事情多久發生一次(once in a while、all the time)，有的點出何時發生或先後順序(from now on、at first、at last)，還有的描述程度深淺(more or less、to some extent)。它們大多是固定搭配，介系詞和字序都不能隨意更改，最好連同例句整組背下來。特別留意意思相近或形近的片語(如 at first／at last、on time／in time)，別在克漏字裡選錯。

idiom.title: 慣用語與諺語
idiom.summary: 慣用語(idioms)和諺語(proverbs)是英文中「字面意思」跟「真正意思」不同的固定說法，例如 a piece of cake 不是真的蛋糕，而是「輕而易舉的事」。會考和學測常在閱讀、克漏字或翻譯中考這類片語，死背字面往往會答錯。學習時要整組記下來，並搭配例句理解使用情境，才能又快又準地判讀。

daily.title: 生活情境常用片語
daily.summary: 生活情境常用片語是英語會話與寫作中出現頻率極高的「固定搭配」，它們的意思往往不能只靠單字字面拆解，而要整組記憶。掌握這些片語能讓你聽懂日常對話、寫出自然道地的句子，也是國中會考與高中學測克漏字、閱讀測驗的常見考點。建議搭配例句與語境一起背，多開口練習，久了就能自然脫口而出。

questions.stem: Could you ___ my little brother for an hour? I have to go out.
questions.explanation: look after：照顧、照料。例：My grandmother looks after our dog while we are at school.

questions.stem: Even after failing three times, she refused to ___ her dream.
questions.explanation: give up：放棄。例：Don't give up now; you are almost at the finish line.

questions.stem: Never ___ until tomorrow what you can do today.
questions.explanation: put off：拖延、延期。例：We had to put off the sports day because of the heavy rain.

questions.stem: The printer stopped because it had ___ ink.
questions.explanation: run out of：用完、耗盡。例：We ran out of milk, so I went to buy some more.

questions.stem: It's important to ___ the people you work with every day.
questions.explanation: get along with：與(某人)相處融洽。例：She gets along well with all of her classmates.

questions.stem: The students are ___ going on the school trip next week.
questions.explanation: look forward to：期待、盼望。例：I'm looking forward to the summer vacation.

questions.stem: While our parents are away, my sister will ___ the house.
questions.explanation: take care of：照顧、處理。例：You should take care of your health during the exam week.

questions.stem: The room is too hot, so please ___ the fan to cool us down.
questions.explanation: turn on：打開(電器、開關)。例：It's getting dark; please turn on the light.

questions.stem: To save energy, please ___ the lights when you leave.
questions.explanation: turn off：關掉(電器、開關)。例：Don't forget to turn off the TV before you go to bed.

questions.stem: Please ___ the toys on the floor before dinner.
questions.explanation: pick up：接送、撿起、學會。例：My dad picks me up from school every afternoon.

questions.stem: Let's search online to ___ what time the movie starts.
questions.explanation: find out：查明、發現(真相、資訊)。例：I want to find out how the accident happened.

questions.stem: She spent the whole morning ___ a birthday gift for her mom.
questions.explanation: look for：尋找。例：I'm looking for my keys; have you seen them?

questions.stem: I don't know this word, so let me ___ its meaning in the dictionary.
questions.explanation: look up：查閱(字典、資料)。例：If you don't know the word, look it up in a dictionary.

questions.stem: The teacher promised to ___ the problem and give us an answer.
questions.explanation: look into：調查、研究。例：The police are looking into the cause of the fire.

questions.stem: When I ___, I want to become a doctor.
questions.explanation: grow up：長大、成長。例：He grew up in a small town near the sea.

questions.stem: The alarm rang, but he was too tired to ___.
questions.explanation: get up：起床、起身。例：I usually get up at six thirty on school days.

questions.stem: Please ___ me ___ at seven; I have an early class.
questions.explanation: wake up：醒來、叫醒。例：The loud noise woke me up in the middle of the night.

questions.stem: Before the game, the players ___ their uniforms.
questions.explanation: put on：穿上、戴上。例：Put on your jacket; it's cold outside.

questions.stem: The plane will ___ in ten minutes, so fasten your seat belt.
questions.explanation: take off：脫下;(飛機)起飛。例：Please take off your shoes before entering the room.

questions.stem: Remember to ___ the money I lent you yesterday.
questions.explanation: give back：歸還。例：Please give back the book you borrowed last week.

questions.stem: The teacher asked us to ___ our reports before class ends.
questions.explanation: hand in：繳交、上繳。例：You must hand in your homework by Friday.

questions.stem: Volunteers will ___ free water to the runners along the road.
questions.explanation: hand out：發放、分發。例：The teacher handed out the test papers to the students.

questions.stem: You need to ___ the blanks with the correct words.
questions.explanation: fill in：填寫(表格、空格)。例：Please fill in your name and phone number on the form.

questions.stem: Please don't ___ the paper; I still need it later.
questions.explanation: throw away：丟棄。例：Don't throw away those bottles; we can recycle them.

questions.stem: Please ___ your books before we start the test.
questions.explanation: put away：收好、放回原處。例：After playing, the children put away their toys.

questions.stem: He had to ___ the job offer because it was too far from home.
questions.explanation: turn down：調小(音量);拒絕。例：Could you turn down the music? It's too loud.

questions.stem: We waited for an hour, but he never ___ at the meeting.
questions.explanation: turn up：調大(音量);出現。例：Please turn up the volume; I can't hear the news.

questions.stem: Where do you ___? I can't tell from your accent.
questions.explanation: come from：來自、出身於。例：My new classmate comes from Japan.

questions.stem: Our team needs to ___ a plan before the meeting.
questions.explanation: come up with：想出、提出(點子)。例：She came up with a great idea for the school festival.

questions.stem: While reading, I ___ a word I had never seen before.
questions.explanation: come across：偶然遇到、無意間發現。例：I came across an old photo while cleaning my room.

questions.stem: Children often ___ their parents for support.
questions.explanation: depend on：依賴、取決於。例：Whether we go hiking depends on the weather.

questions.stem: She was excited to ___ the school's dance performance.
questions.explanation: take part in：參加、參與。例：Many students took part in the speech contest.

questions.stem: The graduation ceremony will ___ next Saturday morning.
questions.explanation: take place：發生、舉行。例：The concert will take place in the city hall.

questions.stem: Please ___ the road signs while you are driving.
questions.explanation: pay attention to：注意、留意。例：You should pay attention to the teacher in class.

questions.stem: He is very ___ math, so he always gets high scores.
questions.explanation: be good at：擅長於。例：My sister is good at playing the piano.

questions.stem: She is really ___ art, so she visits museums often.
questions.explanation: be interested in：對……有興趣。例：I am interested in learning about space and stars.

questions.stem: He is ___ dogs because one bit him when he was young.
questions.explanation: be afraid of：害怕……。例：Many children are afraid of the dark.

questions.stem: The ring is ___ gold, so it is quite expensive.
questions.explanation: be made of：由……製成(看得出原料)。例：This table is made of wood.

questions.stem: Most students ___ the teacher about the new rule.
questions.explanation: agree with：同意(某人)。例：I agree with you that we should start earlier.

questions.stem: The old machine ___ again, so no one could use it.
questions.explanation: break down：故障、拋錨;崩潰。例：Our car broke down on the way to the beach.

questions.stem: The couple decided to ___ because they always argued.
questions.explanation: break up：分手;解散。例：They broke up after dating for two years.

questions.stem: The train has arrived, so let's ___ and find our seats.
questions.explanation: get on：上車(公車、火車、飛機)。例：We got on the bus and found two empty seats.

questions.stem: This is my stop, so I have to ___ the bus right now.
questions.explanation: get off：下車。例：You should get off the bus at the next stop.

questions.stem: It's not easy to ___ the fear of speaking in public.
questions.explanation: get over：克服、從……中復原。例：It took her a month to get over the flu.

questions.stem: The government will ___ a new policy next year.
questions.explanation: carry out：執行、實行(計畫)。例：The team worked hard to carry out the plan.

questions.stem: The students helped to ___ the stage for the show.
questions.explanation: set up：設立、建立、架設。例：They set up a small shop near the station.

questions.stem: It can be hard to ___ so much stress before exams.
questions.explanation: deal with：處理、應付。例：A good leader knows how to deal with problems.

questions.stem: Teachers should never have to ___ students shouting in class.
questions.explanation: put up with：忍受、容忍。例：I can't put up with his rudeness any longer.

questions.stem: He tends to ___ anyone who didn't go to a famous university.
questions.explanation: look down on：看不起、輕視。例：You shouldn't look down on people just because they are poor.

questions.stem: Students naturally ___ teachers who are both kind and knowledgeable.
questions.explanation: look up to：尊敬、敬仰。例：Many young athletes look up to this legendary player.

questions.stem: No amount of money can ___ the time we lost.
questions.explanation: make up for：彌補、補償。例：He worked overtime to make up for the days he was absent.

questions.stem: We finally decided to ___ all the old furniture in the garage.
questions.explanation: get rid of：擺脫、除掉。例：It's hard to get rid of a bad habit once it forms.

questions.stem: To stay healthy, you should ___ fast food and fried snacks.
questions.explanation: cut down on：減少(用量、攝取)。例：The doctor advised him to cut down on sugar and salt.

questions.stem: Can you help me ___ why my computer keeps crashing?
questions.explanation: figure out：弄清楚、想通、算出。例：It took me a while to figure out how the machine works.

questions.stem: When the manager retired, her assistant ___ the whole department.
questions.explanation: take over：接管、接手。例：His son will take over the family business next year.

questions.stem: Advances in technology have ___ great changes in our daily lives.
questions.explanation: bring about：帶來、造成、引發。例：The new policy brought about many changes in the school.

questions.stem: Everyone was worried, but things ___ fine in the end.
questions.explanation: turn out：結果是、原來是。例：The movie turned out to be much better than we expected.

questions.stem: The concert was ___ for good; ticket holders got full refunds.
questions.explanation: call off：取消。例：They had to call off the picnic because of the heavy rain.

questions.stem: The government refused to ___ to the terrorists' demands, no matter the pressure.
questions.explanation: give in：讓步、屈服。例：His parents finally gave in and let him go to the concert.

questions.stem: On my way home, I ___ my former teacher by chance.

questions.stem: The chef prepared the sauce ___ so it would be ready for dinner.
questions.explanation: ahead of time：提前、事先。例：We arrived at the station ahead of time to catch the early train.

questions.stem: ___, there are no tickets left for the concert.
questions.explanation: at present：目前、現在。例：At present, she is studying abroad in Japan.

questions.stem: People read fewer paper books ___ because of the Internet.
questions.explanation: these days：最近、如今。例：These days, most students carry smartphones.

questions.stem: As the exam got closer, she became ___ nervous.
questions.explanation: more and more：越來越（多）。例：More and more people are learning to cook at home.

questions.stem: I have read three chapters ___, and the story is exciting.
questions.explanation: so far：到目前為止。例：So far, the plan is working well.

questions.stem: ___, the movie was good, but the ending was disappointing.
questions.explanation: to some extent：在某種程度上。例：To some extent, I agree with your opinion.

questions.stem: I haven't seen my cousin ___; she has been very busy.
questions.explanation: for a while：一陣子、暫時。例：Let's rest for a while before we continue.

questions.stem: Don't try to do everything ___; take one task at a time.
questions.explanation: all at once：突然；同時一起。例：All at once, the lights went out and the room turned dark.

questions.stem: Keep practicing, and ___ you will be able to swim well.
questions.explanation: before long：不久之後、很快。例：Be patient; before long, spring will come.

questions.stem: ___, the old couple planted rice in the same field.
questions.explanation: year after year：年復一年。例：Year after year, the swallows return to the same rooftop.

questions.stem: We sent the letter last week, so they must have received it ___.
questions.explanation: by now：到現在、如今。例：She left an hour ago, so she should be home by now.

questions.stem: The report is urgent, so please finish it ___.
questions.explanation: as soon as possible：儘快。例：Please reply to my email as soon as possible.

questions.stem: The weather here is nice, though ___ it can be quite cold.
questions.explanation: at times：有時、偶爾。例：At times, I feel nervous before a big test.

questions.stem: ___, his good grades were the result of hard work.
questions.explanation: to a great extent：在很大程度上。例：To a great extent, our success depends on teamwork.

questions.stem: Don't worry about the interview. For someone as prepared as you, it will be ___.
questions.explanation: a piece of cake：輕而易舉的事;小菜一碟。例：The math test was a piece of cake because I had studied hard all week.

questions.stem: Nobody was talking at the party, so I told a joke to ___.
questions.explanation: break the ice：打破僵局;破冰(緩和陌生氣氛)。例：To break the ice, the new teacher asked each student to share a funny story.

questions.stem: He almost never eats fast food; he has a burger only ___.
questions.explanation: once in a blue moon：千載難逢;極少發生。例：My cousin lives abroad, so we only see each other once in a blue moon.

questions.stem: You look pale. Are you feeling ___? Maybe you should see a doctor.
questions.explanation: under the weather：身體不舒服;不太對勁。例：I'm feeling a bit under the weather today, so I'll stay home and rest.

questions.stem: Please ___ the baby for a moment; I need to answer the phone.
questions.explanation: keep an eye on：看顧;留意;盯著。例：Could you keep an eye on my bag while I go to the restroom?

questions.stem: Stop hesitating! You have to ___ before the deadline tomorrow.
questions.explanation: make up one's mind：下定決心;做出決定。例：After thinking for days, Amy finally made up her mind to study abroad.

questions.stem: She planned to ask her teacher a question, but she ___ and stayed silent.
questions.explanation: get cold feet：臨陣退縮;突然膽怯。例：He wanted to give a speech, but he got cold feet at the last minute.

questions.stem: Everyone's in the car and the bags are packed, so let's ___!
questions.explanation: hit the road：出發;上路。例：It's getting late, so we'd better hit the road before the traffic gets worse.

questions.stem: Did you really win the lottery, or are you ___?
questions.explanation: pull one's leg：開某人玩笑;逗某人。例：Don't take it seriously—he's just pulling your leg.

questions.stem: He apologized a week later, but as they say, ___.
questions.explanation: better late than never：遲到總比不到好;亡羊補牢。例：You finally started exercising at fifty—well, better late than never.

questions.stem: Our team practices every morning, ___, even during the typhoon season.
questions.explanation: rain or shine：風雨無阻;不論晴雨。例：The mailman delivers letters every day, rain or shine.

questions.stem: I'd love to travel to Europe, but the plane tickets ___.
options: cost an arm and a leg
questions.explanation: cost an arm and a leg：花大錢;所費不貲。例：That brand-new smartphone costs an arm and a leg.

questions.stem: If you want to pass the entrance exam, you'd better start to ___ now.
questions.explanation: hit the books：用功讀書;開始苦讀。例：Final exams are next week, so it's time to hit the books.

questions.stem: It's already midnight and we're all exhausted, so let's ___.
questions.explanation: call it a day：今天到此為止;收工。例：We've worked for ten hours; let's call it a day.

questions.stem: It was supposed to be a secret gift, but she ___ by mentioning it.
options: let the cat out of the bag
questions.explanation: let the cat out of the bag：不小心洩漏秘密。例：We planned a surprise party, but Tom let the cat out of the bag.

questions.stem: Missing the earlier train was ___, because it later crashed.
questions.explanation: a blessing in disguise：因禍得福;塞翁失馬。例：Losing that job turned out to be a blessing in disguise; I found a better one.

questions.stem: You'll speak English fluently one day; just keep trying, because ___.
questions.explanation: practice makes perfect：熟能生巧。例：Don't give up on the piano—practice makes perfect.

questions.stem: Instead of just saying you love the Earth, start recycling—___.
questions.explanation: actions speak louder than words：行動勝於言語。例：He keeps promising to help, but actions speak louder than words.

questions.stem: If I study English by watching movies, I can relax and learn, ___.
options: kill two birds with one stone
questions.explanation: kill two birds with one stone：一石二鳥;一舉兩得。例：By biking to school, I exercise and save money—killing two birds with one stone.

questions.stem: You should follow the local customs abroad; as the saying goes, ___.
options: when in Rome, do as the Romans do
questions.explanation: when in Rome, do as the Romans do：入境隨俗。例：In Japan, I bow when greeting people—when in Rome, do as the Romans do.

questions.stem: Cheer up after the loss; remember that ___.
options: the early bird catches the worm
every cloud has a silver lining
questions.explanation: every cloud has a silver lining：否極泰來;黑暗中總有希望。例：You failed once, but every cloud has a silver lining—you learned a lot.

questions.stem: Sign up as soon as registration opens; ___.
options: every cloud has a silver lining
the early bird catches the worm
questions.explanation: the early bird catches the worm：早起的鳥兒有蟲吃;先下手為強。例：She arrived first and got the best seat—the early bird catches the worm.

questions.stem: The restaurant looks plain, yet the food is amazing, so ___.
options: don't judge a book by its cover
questions.explanation: don't judge a book by its cover：別以貌取人。例：He looks tough but is very kind—don't judge a book by its cover.

questions.stem: You tell me to quit playing games and focus, but that's ___.
questions.explanation: easier said than done：說起來容易做起來難。例：Saving money every month is easier said than done.

questions.stem: I know the trip was canceled, but ___: now we can save the money.
questions.explanation: look on the bright side：往好處想;樂觀看待。例：You lost the game, but look on the bright side—you made new friends.

questions.stem: He promised to return the book, and he always ___.
questions.explanation: keep one's word：遵守諾言;信守承諾。例：A good leader always keeps his word.

questions.stem: The customer started shouting; he completely ___ over a small mistake.
questions.explanation: lose one's temper：發脾氣;動怒。例：Try not to lose your temper when you disagree with others.

questions.stem: We still have two hours before the movie, so ___ with your lunch.
questions.explanation: take one's time：慢慢來;不用急。例：There's no rush; take your time to finish the drawing.

questions.stem: You skipped class all week; it's time to ___ and talk to the principal.
questions.explanation: face the music：面對現實;承擔後果。例：He broke the window, so now he has to face the music.

questions.stem: Don't spend all your allowance; it's wise to ___.
questions.explanation: save for a rainy day：未雨綢繆;存錢以備不時之需。例：My grandma always tells me to save some money for a rainy day.

questions.stem: Taking six advanced courses at once means you might ___.
options: kill two birds with one stone
bite off more than one can chew
questions.explanation: bite off more than one can chew：貪多嚼不爛;不自量力。例：He joined five clubs and bit off more than he could chew.

questions.stem: We've finished half the exam and had no problems—___.
questions.explanation: so far so good：到目前為止一切順利。例：How's the new project going? So far so good.

questions.stem: If you want strong muscles, you must work out hard—___.
questions.explanation: no pain, no gain：一分耕耘,一分收穫;沒有付出就沒有收穫。例：Training is tough, but no pain, no gain.

questions.stem: Don't say it's impossible; ___, so keep trying.
options: where there's a will, there's a way
questions.explanation: where there's a will, there's a way：有志者事竟成。例：She learned to walk again after the accident—where there's a will, there's a way.

questions.stem: You think you're too old to study coding? Remember, ___.
options: it's never too late to learn
the early bird catches the worm
questions.explanation: it's never too late to learn：學習永遠不嫌晚;活到老學到老。例：My father started learning English at sixty—it's never too late to learn.

questions.stem: Don't feel alone about the workload; we're all ___.
questions.explanation: in the same boat：處境相同;同病相憐。例：We all failed the quiz, so we're in the same boat.

questions.stem: I was so angry at the rude waiter that I gave him ___, telling him exactly how careless he was.
questions.explanation: a piece of one's mind：直言不諱地責備;把不滿說出來。例：When he cut in line, she gave him a piece of her mind.

questions.stem: They've sent you the contract, so ___—it's your turn to reply.
options: the ball is in your court
questions.explanation: the ball is in your court：該由你決定或行動了。例：I've made my offer; now the ball is in your court.

questions.stem: If you ___ while studying, you won't really understand the material.
questions.explanation: cut corners：偷工減料;抄捷徑省事。例：The builder cut corners, so the house fell apart quickly.

questions.stem: After the long meeting, the whole team was finally ___ about the plan.
questions.explanation: on the same page：看法一致;達成共識。例：Before we start, let's make sure everyone is on the same page.

questions.stem: When my neighbor moved house, several friends came to ___.
questions.explanation: lend a hand：幫忙;伸出援手。例：Could you lend a hand with these heavy boxes?

questions.stem: The new year is a good time to ___ and drop your bad habits.
questions.explanation: turn over a new leaf：改過自新;重新開始。例：After failing the class, he decided to turn over a new leaf and study hard.

questions.stem: ___, have you seen my English textbook anywhere?
questions.explanation: by the way：順帶一提;對了。例：By the way, did you finish the science report yet?

questions.stem: You didn't sleep at all last night? ___ you look so pale today.
questions.explanation: no wonder：難怪。例：No wonder you're so tired; you stayed up all night studying.

questions.stem: In this game, all the players must ___ rolling the dice.
questions.explanation: take turns：輪流。例：My sister and I take turns washing the dishes after dinner.

questions.stem: Now that he is in high school, he does his homework ___ without help.
questions.explanation: on one's own：獨自;靠自己。例：She solved the difficult math problem on her own.

questions.stem: This sentence doesn't ___; I think there is a grammar mistake.
questions.explanation: make sense：有道理;說得通。例：Your explanation finally makes sense to me now.

questions.stem: I'll ___ the teacher to ask about the exam schedule.
questions.explanation: get in touch with：與……聯絡。例：Please get in touch with me if you have any questions.

questions.stem: ___ at the party tonight, but remember to come home before ten.
questions.explanation: have fun：玩得開心。例：We had a lot of fun at the beach last weekend.

questions.stem: She kept saying no, and nothing we said could make her ___.
questions.explanation: change one's mind：改變主意。例：I wanted pizza, but I changed my mind and ordered noodles.

questions.stem: After years of practice, her wish to become a singer finally ___.
questions.explanation: come true：實現;成真。例：If you work hard, your dream will come true one day.

questions.stem: I know the exam is hard, but I will ___ to pass it.
questions.explanation: do one's best：盡力;全力以赴。例：Just do your best, and don't worry about the result.

questions.stem: ___ that being polite to others is always important.
questions.explanation: keep in mind：記住;牢記。例：Please keep in mind that the library closes at nine.

questions.stem: Look at the actors getting ready—they seem to ___ start the play.
questions.explanation: be about to：即將;正要。例：I was about to leave when the phone rang.

questions.stem: ___ you can borrow my pen; just give it back later.
questions.explanation: of course：當然。例："Can you help me?" "Of course I can!"

questions.stem: Good parents ___ their children's health and happiness.
questions.explanation: care about：在乎;關心。例：She really cares about her friends' feelings.

questions.stem: After a month, she finally ___ waking up early.
questions.explanation: get used to：習慣於。例：It took me a while to get used to the new school.

questions.stem: Joining a club is a great way to ___ at a new school.
questions.explanation: make friends：交朋友。例：It's easy to make friends when you are friendly.

questions.stem: ___! Things will get better after this hard week.
questions.explanation: cheer up：使振作;打起精神。例：I brought you some cake to cheer you up.

questions.stem: Everyone has a different ___ about what makes a good movie.
questions.explanation: point of view：觀點;看法。例：Try to see the problem from your friend's point of view.

vocab.example: My grandmother looks after our dog while we are at school.
vocab.exampleZh: 我們上學時,奶奶會幫忙照顧我們的狗。

vocab.example: Don't give up now; you are almost at the finish line.
vocab.exampleZh: 現在別放棄,你快到終點線了。

vocab.example: We had to put off the sports day because of the heavy rain.
vocab.exampleZh: 因為下大雨,我們只好把運動會延期。

vocab.example: We ran out of milk, so I went to buy some more.
vocab.exampleZh: 我們的牛奶用完了,所以我去多買了一些。

vocab.example: She gets along well with all of her classmates.
vocab.exampleZh: 她和所有同學都相處得很好。

vocab.example: I'm looking forward to the summer vacation.
vocab.exampleZh: 我很期待暑假的到來。

vocab.example: You should take care of your health during the exam week.
vocab.exampleZh: 考試週期間你應該照顧好自己的健康。

vocab.example: It's getting dark; please turn on the light.
vocab.exampleZh: 天色變暗了,請打開電燈。

vocab.example: Don't forget to turn off the TV before you go to bed.
vocab.exampleZh: 睡覺前別忘了關掉電視。

vocab.example: My dad picks me up from school every afternoon.
vocab.exampleZh: 我爸爸每天下午都到學校接我。

vocab.example: I want to find out how the accident happened.
vocab.exampleZh: 我想查明這場意外是怎麼發生的。

vocab.example: I'm looking for my keys; have you seen them?
vocab.exampleZh: 我在找我的鑰匙,你有看到嗎?

vocab.example: If you don't know the word, look it up in a dictionary.
vocab.exampleZh: 如果你不懂這個字,就去字典裡查。

vocab.example: The police are looking into the cause of the fire.
vocab.exampleZh: 警方正在調查這場火災的起因。

vocab.example: He grew up in a small town near the sea.
vocab.exampleZh: 他在海邊的一個小鎮長大。

vocab.example: I usually get up at six thirty on school days.
vocab.exampleZh: 上學日我通常六點半起床。

vocab.example: The loud noise woke me up in the middle of the night.
vocab.exampleZh: 那巨大的聲響在半夜把我吵醒。

vocab.example: Put on your jacket; it's cold outside.
vocab.exampleZh: 穿上你的外套,外面很冷。

vocab.example: Please take off your shoes before entering the room.
vocab.exampleZh: 進房間前請脫掉鞋子。

vocab.example: Please give back the book you borrowed last week.
vocab.exampleZh: 請歸還你上週借的那本書。

vocab.example: You must hand in your homework by Friday.
vocab.exampleZh: 你必須在星期五前繳交作業。

vocab.example: The teacher handed out the test papers to the students.
vocab.exampleZh: 老師把考卷發給學生。

vocab.example: Please fill in your name and phone number on the form.
vocab.exampleZh: 請在表格上填寫你的姓名和電話。

vocab.example: Don't throw away those bottles; we can recycle them.
vocab.exampleZh: 別把那些瓶子丟掉,我們可以回收。

vocab.example: After playing, the children put away their toys.
vocab.exampleZh: 玩完後,孩子們把玩具收好。

vocab.example: Could you turn down the music? It's too loud.
vocab.exampleZh: 你可以把音樂調小聲一點嗎?太大聲了。

vocab.example: Please turn up the volume; I can't hear the news.
vocab.exampleZh: 請把音量調大,我聽不到新聞。

vocab.example: My new classmate comes from Japan.
vocab.exampleZh: 我的新同學來自日本。

vocab.example: She came up with a great idea for the school festival.
vocab.exampleZh: 她為校慶想出了一個很棒的點子。

vocab.example: I came across an old photo while cleaning my room.
vocab.exampleZh: 我在整理房間時偶然發現一張舊照片。

vocab.example: Whether we go hiking depends on the weather.
vocab.exampleZh: 我們是否去健行取決於天氣。

vocab.example: Many students took part in the speech contest.
vocab.exampleZh: 許多學生參加了演講比賽。

vocab.example: The concert will take place in the city hall.
vocab.exampleZh: 這場音樂會將在市政廳舉行。

vocab.example: You should pay attention to the teacher in class.
vocab.exampleZh: 上課時你應該注意聽老師講。

vocab.example: My sister is good at playing the piano.
vocab.exampleZh: 我姊姊擅長彈鋼琴。

vocab.example: I am interested in learning about space and stars.
vocab.exampleZh: 我對學習太空和星星很有興趣。

vocab.example: Many children are afraid of the dark.
vocab.exampleZh: 許多小孩都怕黑。

vocab.example: This table is made of wood.
vocab.exampleZh: 這張桌子是木頭做的。

vocab.example: I agree with you that we should start earlier.
vocab.exampleZh: 我同意你的看法,我們應該早點開始。

vocab.example: Our car broke down on the way to the beach.
vocab.exampleZh: 我們的車在往海邊的路上拋錨了。

vocab.example: They broke up after dating for two years.
vocab.exampleZh: 他們交往兩年後分手了。

vocab.example: We got on the bus and found two empty seats.
vocab.exampleZh: 我們上了公車,找到兩個空位。

vocab.example: You should get off the bus at the next stop.
vocab.exampleZh: 你應該在下一站下公車。

vocab.example: It took her a month to get over the flu.
vocab.exampleZh: 她花了一個月才從流感中康復。

vocab.example: The team worked hard to carry out the plan.
vocab.exampleZh: 團隊努力執行這項計畫。

vocab.example: They set up a small shop near the station.
vocab.exampleZh: 他們在車站附近開了一家小店。

vocab.example: A good leader knows how to deal with problems.
vocab.exampleZh: 好的領導者懂得如何處理問題。

vocab.example: I can't put up with his rudeness any longer.
vocab.exampleZh: 我再也無法忍受他的無禮了。

vocab.example: You shouldn't look down on people just because they are poor.
vocab.exampleZh: 你不該只因為別人貧窮就看不起他們。

vocab.example: Many young athletes look up to this legendary player.
vocab.exampleZh: 許多年輕運動員都很敬仰這位傳奇球員。

vocab.example: He worked overtime to make up for the days he was absent.
vocab.exampleZh: 他加班來彌補他請假缺席的日子。

vocab.example: It's hard to get rid of a bad habit once it forms.
vocab.exampleZh: 壞習慣一旦養成就很難擺脫。

vocab.example: The doctor advised him to cut down on sugar and salt.
vocab.exampleZh: 醫生建議他減少糖和鹽的攝取。

vocab.example: It took me a while to figure out how the machine works.
vocab.exampleZh: 我花了一點時間才弄清楚這台機器怎麼運作。

vocab.example: His son will take over the family business next year.
vocab.exampleZh: 他兒子明年將接手家族事業。

vocab.example: The new policy brought about many changes in the school.
vocab.exampleZh: 這項新政策為學校帶來了許多改變。

vocab.example: The movie turned out to be much better than we expected.
vocab.exampleZh: 這部電影結果比我們預期的好看多了。

vocab.example: They had to call off the picnic because of the heavy rain.
vocab.exampleZh: 因為大雨，他們只好取消野餐。

vocab.example: His parents finally gave in and let him go to the concert.
vocab.exampleZh: 他父母最後讓步，讓他去聽演唱會。

vocab.example: I ran into an old friend at the supermarket yesterday.
vocab.exampleZh: 我昨天在超市巧遇一位老朋友。

vocab.example: She takes after her mother in both looks and temper.
vocab.exampleZh: 她的長相和脾氣都像她媽媽。

vocab.example: She was brought up by her grandparents in the countryside.
vocab.exampleZh: 她是由祖父母在鄉下養大的。

vocab.example: The teacher pointed out several mistakes in my essay.
vocab.exampleZh: 老師指出了我作文中的幾個錯誤。

vocab.example: If you study hard, you'll soon catch up with the others.
vocab.exampleZh: 如果你努力用功，很快就能趕上其他人。

vocab.example: It's hard to keep up with the latest technology these days.
vocab.exampleZh: 如今要跟上最新科技很不容易。

vocab.example: The store gave away free samples to attract customers.
vocab.exampleZh: 這家店免費送出試用品以吸引顧客。

vocab.example: A fire broke out in the factory late at night.
vocab.exampleZh: 深夜工廠發生了火災。

vocab.example: We set off early in the morning to avoid the traffic.
vocab.exampleZh: 我們一大早就出發以避開車潮。

vocab.example: He couldn't account for the missing money.
vocab.exampleZh: 他無法解釋這筆不見的錢的去向。

vocab.example: The mayor called on citizens to save water during the drought.
vocab.exampleZh: 市長呼籲市民在乾旱期間節約用水。

vocab.example: He insisted on paying the bill for everyone.
vocab.exampleZh: 他堅持要幫大家付帳。

vocab.example: The letters UN stand for the United Nations.
vocab.exampleZh: UN 這兩個字母代表聯合國。

vocab.example: When she has a problem, she always turns to her best friend.
vocab.exampleZh: 每當遇到問題，她總是向最好的朋友求助。

vocab.example: I'm sure we can work out a solution together.
vocab.exampleZh: 我相信我們能一起想出解決辦法。

vocab.example: My parents are proud of me for winning the speech contest.
vocab.exampleZh: 我贏得演講比賽，爸媽以我為榮。

vocab.example: Tainan is famous for its delicious traditional snacks.
vocab.exampleZh: 台南以美味的傳統小吃聞名。

vocab.example: She is worried about her final exam next week.
vocab.exampleZh: 她擔心下週的期末考。

vocab.example: Drivers should be aware of children crossing the street near schools.
vocab.exampleZh: 駕駛人在學校附近應留意過馬路的孩童。

vocab.example: With enough practice, you are capable of solving even the hardest problems.
vocab.exampleZh: 只要多練習，你有能力解出最難的題目。

vocab.example: Each student is responsible for keeping the classroom clean.
vocab.exampleZh: 每位學生都要負責維持教室整潔。

vocab.example: My grandmother is fond of gardening and grows many flowers.
vocab.exampleZh: 我奶奶喜愛園藝，種了許多花。

vocab.example: I am tired of eating the same lunch every day.
vocab.exampleZh: 我厭倦了每天吃一樣的午餐。

vocab.example: The park is full of people on sunny weekends.
vocab.exampleZh: 晴朗的週末公園裡擠滿了人。

vocab.example: This song is similar to one I heard last year.
vocab.exampleZh: 這首歌和我去年聽過的一首很像。

vocab.example: Life in the countryside is different from life in the city.
vocab.exampleZh: 鄉下的生活和城市的生活不一樣。

vocab.example: Regular exercise is good for your health.
vocab.exampleZh: 規律運動對你的健康有益。

vocab.example: Staying up late is bad for your body.
vocab.exampleZh: 熬夜對你的身體有害。

vocab.example: My mom was angry with me for coming home late.
vocab.exampleZh: 我晚回家，媽媽對我很生氣。

vocab.example: The customers were angry about the poor service.
vocab.exampleZh: 顧客對差勁的服務感到生氣。

vocab.example: I felt sorry for the lost dog standing in the rain.
vocab.exampleZh: 我為那隻站在雨中迷路的狗感到難過。

vocab.example: My neighbors have always been good to my family.
vocab.exampleZh: 我的鄰居一直對我家人很好。

vocab.example: We should be kind to animals and never hurt them.
vocab.exampleZh: 我們應該善待動物，絕不傷害牠們。

vocab.example: He is famous as a scientist who never stops asking questions.
vocab.exampleZh: 他以一位不斷發問的科學家著稱。

vocab.example: We were surprised at how quickly he finished the test.
vocab.exampleZh: 他這麼快就寫完考試，我們很驚訝。

vocab.example: She is good with children, so she makes a great teacher.
vocab.exampleZh: 她很會帶小孩，所以是很棒的老師。

vocab.example: Are you ready for the big game this afternoon?
vocab.exampleZh: 你準備好迎接今天下午的大賽了嗎?

vocab.example: After living abroad for years, he is used to speaking English every day.
vocab.exampleZh: 在國外住了幾年後，他習慣了每天說英文。

vocab.example: The night market was crowded with tourists.
vocab.exampleZh: 夜市裡擠滿了觀光客。

vocab.example: Paper is made from trees.
vocab.exampleZh: 紙是由樹木製成的。

vocab.example: In winter, the mountains are covered with snow.
vocab.exampleZh: 冬天時，山上被白雪覆蓋。

vocab.example: He was absent from school because of a bad cold.
vocab.exampleZh: 他因為重感冒而沒去上學。

vocab.example: This teacher is popular with her students.
vocab.exampleZh: 這位老師很受學生歡迎。

vocab.example: The movie is based on a true story.
vocab.exampleZh: 這部電影根據真實故事改編。

vocab.example: She is busy with her science project this week.
vocab.exampleZh: 她這週忙著做科學報告。

vocab.example: We are worried for the hikers lost in the mountains.
vocab.exampleZh: 我們為在山中迷路的登山客擔心。

vocab.example: The teacher was pleased with our performance.
vocab.exampleZh: 老師對我們的表現感到滿意。

vocab.example: Customers are satisfied with the quality of the products.
vocab.exampleZh: 顧客對產品的品質感到滿意。

vocab.example: This museum is worth visiting when you travel to Taipei.
vocab.exampleZh: 到台北旅遊時，這座博物館值得一看。

vocab.example: Heavy rain in the afternoon is typical of summer in Taiwan.
vocab.exampleZh: 午後大雨是台灣夏天的典型天氣。

vocab.example: This movie is suitable for children of all ages.
vocab.exampleZh: 這部電影適合各年齡的孩童觀看。

vocab.example: Kids are curious about how things work.
vocab.exampleZh: 孩子們對事物如何運作感到好奇。

vocab.example: He was jealous of his friend's new bike.
vocab.exampleZh: 他嫉妒朋友的新腳踏車。

vocab.example: She is confident of passing the entrance exam.
vocab.exampleZh: 她有信心通過入學考試。

vocab.example: Young children are dependent on their parents.
vocab.exampleZh: 幼小的孩子依賴父母。

vocab.example: He was ashamed of lying to his parents.
vocab.exampleZh: 他為對父母說謊感到羞愧。

vocab.example: I am sure of my answer to the last question.
vocab.exampleZh: 我對最後一題的答案很有把握。

vocab.example: The students are excited about the school trip next week.
vocab.exampleZh: 學生們對下週的校外教學感到興奮。

vocab.example: Ms. Lin is in charge of the school library this semester.
vocab.exampleZh: 林老師這學期負責管理學校圖書館。

vocab.example: I didn't break the vase on purpose; it was an accident.
vocab.exampleZh: 我不是故意打破花瓶的，那是意外。

vocab.example: I met my old classmate by accident at the train station.
vocab.exampleZh: 我在火車站偶然遇見了老同學。

vocab.example: Many wild animals are in danger of extinction because of pollution.
vocab.exampleZh: 許多野生動物因污染而面臨滅絕的危險。

vocab.example: Please book the tickets in advance so we can get better seats.
vocab.exampleZh: 請事先訂票，這樣我們能坐到比較好的位子。

vocab.example: The train arrived on time, so we didn't miss our connection.
vocab.exampleZh: 火車準時抵達，所以我們沒錯過轉車。

vocab.example: We got to the airport just in time to catch our flight.
vocab.exampleZh: 我們及時趕到機場，剛好趕上班機。

vocab.example: You should sleep at least eight hours a day to stay healthy.
vocab.exampleZh: 你每天至少要睡八小時才能保持健康。

vocab.example: The whole test will take an hour at most, so don't worry.
vocab.exampleZh: 整場考試最多只需一小時，別擔心。

vocab.example: In general, students in this school do well in English.
vocab.exampleZh: 一般而言，這所學校的學生英文表現不錯。

vocab.example: She gave up the job in the city for the sake of her family.
vocab.exampleZh: 她為了家人放棄了城市裡的工作。

vocab.example: In terms of price, this phone is the best choice for students.
vocab.exampleZh: 就價格而言，這支手機是學生最好的選擇。

vocab.example: On behalf of our class, I want to thank you for your help.
vocab.exampleZh: 我代表全班，感謝您的幫忙。

vocab.example: Most students are in favor of a longer lunch break.
vocab.exampleZh: 大多數學生贊成延長午休時間。

vocab.example: After the earthquake, many families were in need of food and water.
vocab.exampleZh: 地震後，許多家庭需要食物和水。

vocab.example: In spite of the heavy rain, they still went hiking.
vocab.exampleZh: 儘管下著大雨，他們還是去健行了。

vocab.example: The game was canceled because of the storm.
vocab.exampleZh: 比賽因暴風雨而取消。

vocab.example: Let's take the bus instead of walking in this heat.
vocab.exampleZh: 這麼熱，我們搭公車吧，別用走的。

vocab.example: People share ideas by means of the Internet every day.
vocab.exampleZh: 人們每天透過網路分享想法。

vocab.example: A tall tree stands in front of our house.
vocab.exampleZh: 我們家前面有一棵高大的樹。

vocab.example: The phone rang in the middle of the movie.
vocab.exampleZh: 電影演到一半時電話響了。

vocab.example: The library is at the end of this hallway.
vocab.exampleZh: 圖書館在這條走廊的盡頭。

vocab.example: They argued for hours, but in the end they reached an agreement.
vocab.exampleZh: 他們爭論了好幾個小時，但最後達成了共識。

vocab.example: At first the math seemed hard, but later it became easy.
vocab.exampleZh: 起初數學看起來很難，後來就變簡單了。

vocab.example: After a long trip, we arrived home at last.
vocab.exampleZh: 經過漫長的旅程，我們終於到家了。

vocab.example: The teacher told us to sit down at once.
vocab.exampleZh: 老師叫我們馬上坐下。

vocab.example: The two sisters have a lot in common, including their love of music.
vocab.exampleZh: 這對姊妹有很多共同點，包括對音樂的熱愛。

vocab.example: I enjoy all sports, tennis in particular.
vocab.exampleZh: 我喜歡所有運動，尤其是網球。

vocab.example: He was too shy to sing in public.
vocab.exampleZh: 他太害羞了，不敢當眾唱歌。

vocab.example: The teacher spoke to the student in private after class.
vocab.exampleZh: 下課後老師私下和那名學生談話。

vocab.example: These shoes are on sale this week, so let's buy a pair.
vocab.exampleZh: 這雙鞋這週特價，我們買一雙吧。

vocab.example: The house across the street is for sale.
vocab.exampleZh: 對街那棟房子正在出售。

vocab.example: The school is close, so I go there on foot every day.
vocab.exampleZh: 學校很近，所以我每天走路去。

vocab.example: Can I pay in cash, or do you only take cards?
vocab.exampleZh: 我可以付現金嗎，還是你們只收卡?

vocab.example: The elevator is out of order, so we have to use the stairs.
vocab.exampleZh: 電梯故障了，所以我們得走樓梯。

vocab.example: This map is out of date; many new roads are missing.
vocab.exampleZh: 這張地圖過時了，少了許多新的道路。

vocab.example: She left in a hurry and forgot her umbrella.
vocab.exampleZh: 她匆匆離開，忘了帶傘。

vocab.example: I took your pen by mistake; here it is.
vocab.exampleZh: 我不小心拿了你的筆，還你。

vocab.example: She got up early in order to catch the first bus.
vocab.exampleZh: 她早起是為了趕搭第一班公車。

vocab.example: As a result of the typhoon, all flights were canceled.
vocab.exampleZh: 由於颱風的緣故，所有航班都取消了。

vocab.example: In addition to English, she can speak French and Japanese.
vocab.exampleZh: 除了英文，她還會說法文和日文。

vocab.example: Thanks to your help, I finished the project on time.
vocab.exampleZh: 多虧你的幫忙，我準時完成了專案。

vocab.example: You can't watch TV and study well at the same time.
vocab.exampleZh: 你沒辦法同時看電視又好好讀書。

vocab.example: In the future, more people may drive electric cars.
vocab.exampleZh: 未來，可能會有更多人開電動車。

vocab.example: Many animals sleep in winter; bears, for example, hibernate for months.
vocab.exampleZh: 許多動物在冬天睡覺，例如熊會冬眠好幾個月。

vocab.example: The city is exciting; on the other hand, it can be very noisy.
vocab.exampleZh: 城市很刺激，但另一方面也可能很吵。

vocab.example: She helped me with my homework, and I bought her lunch in return.
vocab.exampleZh: 她幫我做功課，我請她吃午餐作為回報。

vocab.example: He didn't study hard, and as a result, he failed the exam.
vocab.exampleZh: 他不用功,結果考試不及格。

vocab.example: The hotel is clean. In addition, it is very close to the station.
vocab.exampleZh: 這間旅館很乾淨。此外,它離車站很近。

vocab.example: He is a man of few words; in other words, he doesn't talk much.
vocab.exampleZh: 他話很少;換句話說,他不太愛講話。

vocab.example: In conclusion, reading every day helps us learn new words.
vocab.exampleZh: 總而言之,每天閱讀能幫助我們學習新單字。

vocab.example: Above all, remember to stay calm during the test.
vocab.exampleZh: 最重要的是,考試時記得保持冷靜。

vocab.example: The trip was cheap, and what is more, we had a wonderful time.
vocab.exampleZh: 這趟旅行很便宜,而且我們玩得很愉快。

vocab.example: My brother is quiet. In contrast, my sister loves to talk.
vocab.exampleZh: 我哥哥很安靜。相較之下,我姐姐很愛講話。

vocab.example: The game was canceled due to the heavy rain.
vocab.exampleZh: 比賽因為大雨而取消了。

vocab.example: According to the news, it will be sunny tomorrow.
vocab.exampleZh: 根據新聞,明天會是晴天。

vocab.example: Some animals sleep in winter; for instance, bears hibernate.
vocab.exampleZh: 有些動物在冬天睡覺;舉例來說,熊會冬眠。

vocab.example: The test looked hard, but in fact, it was quite easy.
vocab.exampleZh: 考試看起來很難,但事實上相當容易。

vocab.example: I'm not tired at all; on the contrary, I feel full of energy.
vocab.exampleZh: 我一點也不累;相反地,我覺得精力充沛。

vocab.example: She can speak English as well as Japanese.
vocab.exampleZh: 她會說英語以及日語。

vocab.example: In short, hard work is the key to success.
vocab.exampleZh: 簡言之,努力是成功的關鍵。

vocab.example: First of all, I would like to thank my parents.
vocab.exampleZh: 首先,我要感謝我的父母。

vocab.example: Last but not least, don't forget to bring your umbrella.
vocab.exampleZh: 最後但同樣重要的是,別忘了帶傘。

vocab.example: The first plan was risky. By contrast, the second one was safe.
vocab.exampleZh: 第一個計畫很冒險。相比之下,第二個很安全。

vocab.example: As a matter of fact, I have never been to Japan.
vocab.exampleZh: 事實上,我從來沒去過日本。

vocab.example: You can succeed as long as you keep trying.
vocab.exampleZh: 只要你持續努力,就能成功。

vocab.example: I will call you as soon as I get home.
vocab.exampleZh: 我一到家就會打給你。

vocab.example: She spoke slowly so that everyone could understand.
vocab.exampleZh: 她說得很慢,好讓大家都能聽懂。

vocab.example: I enjoy team sports such as basketball and soccer.
vocab.exampleZh: 我喜歡團隊運動,例如籃球和足球。

vocab.example: The plan is cheap, and more importantly, it is safe.
vocab.exampleZh: 這個計畫很便宜,更重要的是,它很安全。

vocab.example: The movie was boring, and on top of that, the seats were uncomfortable.
vocab.exampleZh: 那部電影很無聊,除此之外,座位也不舒服。

vocab.example: Don't be too hard on him; he is only a child, after all.
vocab.exampleZh: 別對他太嚴厲;畢竟他只是個孩子。

vocab.example: He broke the rules and, as a consequence, was punished.
vocab.exampleZh: 他違反了規定,因此受到處罰。

vocab.example: To begin with, we need to decide on a topic.
vocab.exampleZh: 首先,我們需要決定一個主題。

vocab.example: Plants need water to grow; in the same way, we need food to live.
vocab.exampleZh: 植物需要水才能生長;同樣地,我們需要食物才能生存。

vocab.example: The road is dangerous; for this reason, we drive slowly.
vocab.exampleZh: 這條路很危險;基於這個原因,我們開得很慢。

vocab.example: Needless to say, everyone was excited about the holiday.
vocab.exampleZh: 不用說,大家都對假期感到興奮。

vocab.example: We got lost, and to make matters worse, my phone died.
vocab.exampleZh: 我們迷路了,更糟的是,我的手機沒電了。

vocab.example: Compared with last year, the weather is much colder now.
vocab.exampleZh: 與去年相比,現在的天氣冷多了。

vocab.example: As mentioned above, exercise is good for both body and mind.
vocab.exampleZh: 如上所述,運動對身心都有益。

vocab.example: I don't want to go out; besides, it's raining.
vocab.exampleZh: 我不想出門;而且,外面正在下雨。

vocab.example: From now on, I will study English for thirty minutes every day.
vocab.exampleZh: 從現在起，我每天都會念三十分鐘的英文。

vocab.example: If you keep telling lies, sooner or later people will stop trusting you.
vocab.exampleZh: 如果你一直說謊，別人遲早會不再信任你。

vocab.example: We eat out once in a while, usually on my mom's birthday.
vocab.exampleZh: 我們偶爾會出去吃飯，通常是在我媽生日的時候。

vocab.example: From time to time, my grandmother sends me handwritten letters.
vocab.exampleZh: 我奶奶不時會寄手寫信給我。

vocab.example: We were walking home when all of a sudden it started to rain.
vocab.exampleZh: 我們正走路回家，突然下起雨來。

vocab.example: The homework is more or less finished; I just need to check it.
vocab.exampleZh: 作業差不多做完了，我只需要檢查一下。

vocab.example: Day by day, her English is getting better.
vocab.exampleZh: 她的英文一天天地進步。

vocab.example: Little by little, he saved enough money to buy a bike.
vocab.exampleZh: 他一點一點地存夠了錢買腳踏車。

vocab.example: After the accident, he quit riding motorcycles for good.
vocab.exampleZh: 出事之後，他從此再也不騎機車了。

vocab.example: If we take the MRT, we'll get there in no time.
vocab.exampleZh: 如果我們搭捷運，很快就會到了。

vocab.example: Now and then, we visit our old teacher to say hello.
vocab.exampleZh: 我們偶爾會去探望以前的老師打聲招呼。

vocab.example: My little brother asks questions all the time.
vocab.exampleZh: 我弟弟總是不停地問問題。

vocab.example: When the alarm rang, everyone left the building right away.
vocab.exampleZh: 警報一響，大家立刻離開了大樓。

vocab.example: Studying hard now will pay off in the long run.
vocab.exampleZh: 現在努力用功，長遠來看是會有回報的。

vocab.example: For the time being, we'll stay at my aunt's house.
vocab.exampleZh: 我們暫時會住在阿姨家。

vocab.example: Let's solve this problem once and for all so it never happens again.
vocab.exampleZh: 我們把這個問題徹底解決，讓它不再發生。

vocab.example: I told him again and again to be careful, but he didn't listen.
vocab.exampleZh: 我一再地叫他小心，但他就是不聽。

vocab.example: The teacher explained the math problem step by step.
vocab.exampleZh: 老師一步一步地講解這道數學題。

vocab.example: As usual, she was the first one to arrive at the classroom.
vocab.exampleZh: 像往常一樣，她是第一個到教室的人。

vocab.example: We arrived at the station ahead of time to catch the early train.
vocab.exampleZh: 我們提前到車站，好趕上早班火車。

vocab.example: At present, she is studying abroad in Japan.
vocab.exampleZh: 目前她正在日本留學。

vocab.example: These days, most students carry smartphones.
vocab.exampleZh: 如今大多數學生都帶著智慧型手機。

vocab.example: More and more people are learning to cook at home.
vocab.exampleZh: 越來越多人開始學在家做菜。

vocab.example: So far, the plan is working well.
vocab.exampleZh: 到目前為止，這個計畫進行得很順利。

vocab.example: To some extent, I agree with your opinion.
vocab.exampleZh: 在某種程度上，我同意你的看法。

vocab.example: Let's rest for a while before we continue.
vocab.exampleZh: 我們先休息一下再繼續。

vocab.example: All at once, the lights went out and the room turned dark.
vocab.exampleZh: 突然間，燈全滅了，房間陷入一片黑暗。

vocab.example: Be patient; before long, spring will come.
vocab.exampleZh: 耐心點，不久之後春天就會來臨。

vocab.example: Year after year, the swallows return to the same rooftop.
vocab.exampleZh: 年復一年，燕子回到同一個屋頂。

vocab.example: She left an hour ago, so she should be home by now.
vocab.exampleZh: 她一小時前就走了，現在應該到家了。

vocab.example: Please reply to my email as soon as possible.
vocab.exampleZh: 請儘快回覆我的電子郵件。

vocab.example: At times, I feel nervous before a big test.
vocab.exampleZh: 有時候大考前我會覺得緊張。

vocab.example: To a great extent, our success depends on teamwork.
vocab.exampleZh: 在很大程度上，我們的成功取決於團隊合作。

vocab.zh: 輕而易舉的事;小菜一碟
vocab.example: The math test was a piece of cake because I had studied hard all week.
vocab.exampleZh: 這次數學考試輕而易舉,因為我整個禮拜都很用功。

vocab.zh: 打破僵局;破冰(緩和陌生氣氛)
vocab.example: To break the ice, the new teacher asked each student to share a funny story.
vocab.exampleZh: 為了打破僵局,新老師請每位學生分享一個有趣的故事。

vocab.example: My cousin lives abroad, so we only see each other once in a blue moon.
vocab.exampleZh: 我表哥住在國外,所以我們難得才見一次面。

vocab.example: I'm feeling a bit under the weather today, so I'll stay home and rest.
vocab.exampleZh: 我今天覺得有點不舒服,所以會待在家休息。

vocab.example: Could you keep an eye on my bag while I go to the restroom?
vocab.exampleZh: 我去洗手間時,你可以幫我看一下包包嗎?

vocab.example: After thinking for days, Amy finally made up her mind to study abroad.
vocab.exampleZh: 想了好幾天後,Amy 終於下定決心要出國念書。

vocab.example: He wanted to give a speech, but he got cold feet at the last minute.
vocab.exampleZh: 他本來想上台演講,卻在最後一刻臨陣退縮了。

vocab.example: It's getting late, so we'd better hit the road before the traffic gets worse.
vocab.exampleZh: 時間不早了,我們最好在塞車變嚴重前趕快上路。

vocab.example: Don't take it seriously—he's just pulling your leg.
vocab.exampleZh: 別當真,他只是在跟你開玩笑。

vocab.zh: 遲到總比不到好;亡羊補牢
vocab.example: You finally started exercising at fifty—well, better late than never.
vocab.exampleZh: 你五十歲才開始運動,不過遲做總比不做好。

vocab.example: The mailman delivers letters every day, rain or shine.
vocab.exampleZh: 郵差每天送信,風雨無阻。

vocab.word: cost an arm and a leg
vocab.example: That brand-new smartphone costs an arm and a leg.
vocab.exampleZh: 那支全新的智慧型手機貴得要命。

vocab.example: Final exams are next week, so it's time to hit the books.
vocab.exampleZh: 下週就是期末考,是時候該用功讀書了。

vocab.example: We've worked for ten hours; let's call it a day.
vocab.exampleZh: 我們已經工作十個小時了,今天就到此為止吧。

vocab.word: let the cat out of the bag
vocab.example: We planned a surprise party, but Tom let the cat out of the bag.
vocab.exampleZh: 我們計劃辦驚喜派對,但 Tom 不小心把秘密說溜嘴了。

vocab.example: Losing that job turned out to be a blessing in disguise; I found a better one.
vocab.exampleZh: 丟掉那份工作反而是因禍得福,我找到了更好的工作。

vocab.example: Don't give up on the piano—practice makes perfect.
vocab.exampleZh: 別放棄彈鋼琴,熟能生巧。

vocab.example: He keeps promising to help, but actions speak louder than words.
vocab.exampleZh: 他一直說要幫忙,但行動勝於言語。

vocab.word: kill two birds with one stone
vocab.example: By biking to school, I exercise and save money—killing two birds with one stone.
vocab.exampleZh: 騎腳踏車上學讓我既能運動又能省錢,真是一舉兩得。

vocab.word: when in Rome, do as the Romans do
vocab.example: In Japan, I bow when greeting people—when in Rome, do as the Romans do.
vocab.exampleZh: 在日本我用鞠躬打招呼,入境隨俗嘛。

vocab.word: every cloud has a silver lining
vocab.zh: 否極泰來;黑暗中總有希望
vocab.example: You failed once, but every cloud has a silver lining—you learned a lot.
vocab.exampleZh: 你雖然失敗過一次,但否極泰來,你學到了很多。

vocab.word: the early bird catches the worm
vocab.zh: 早起的鳥兒有蟲吃;先下手為強
vocab.example: She arrived first and got the best seat—the early bird catches the worm.
vocab.exampleZh: 她最早到,搶到了最好的位子,真是早起的鳥兒有蟲吃。

vocab.word: don't judge a book by its cover
vocab.example: He looks tough but is very kind—don't judge a book by its cover.
vocab.exampleZh: 他看起來很兇但其實很善良,別以貌取人。

vocab.example: Saving money every month is easier said than done.
vocab.exampleZh: 每個月存錢說起來容易做起來難。

vocab.example: You lost the game, but look on the bright side—you made new friends.
vocab.exampleZh: 你雖然輸了比賽,但往好處想,你交到了新朋友。

vocab.example: A good leader always keeps his word.
vocab.exampleZh: 一個好的領導者總是信守承諾。

vocab.example: Try not to lose your temper when you disagree with others.
vocab.exampleZh: 當你和別人意見不合時,盡量別發脾氣。

vocab.example: There's no rush; take your time to finish the drawing.
vocab.exampleZh: 別急,慢慢把畫完成。

vocab.example: He broke the window, so now he has to face the music.
vocab.exampleZh: 他打破了窗戶,現在得承擔後果。

vocab.zh: 未雨綢繆;存錢以備不時之需
vocab.example: My grandma always tells me to save some money for a rainy day.
vocab.exampleZh: 我奶奶總是叫我存點錢以備不時之需。

vocab.word: bite off more than one can chew
vocab.example: He joined five clubs and bit off more than he could chew.
vocab.exampleZh: 他參加了五個社團,結果貪多嚼不爛。

vocab.example: How's the new project going? So far so good.
vocab.exampleZh: 新專案進行得如何?到目前為止一切順利。

vocab.zh: 一分耕耘,一分收穫;沒有付出就沒有收穫
vocab.example: Training is tough, but no pain, no gain.
vocab.exampleZh: 訓練很辛苦,但沒有付出就沒有收穫。

vocab.word: where there's a will, there's a way
vocab.example: She learned to walk again after the accident—where there's a will, there's a way.
vocab.exampleZh: 她在意外後重新學會走路,真是有志者事竟成。

vocab.word: it's never too late to learn
vocab.zh: 學習永遠不嫌晚;活到老學到老
vocab.example: My father started learning English at sixty—it's never too late to learn.
vocab.exampleZh: 我爸六十歲才開始學英文,學習永遠不嫌晚。

vocab.example: We all failed the quiz, so we're in the same boat.
vocab.exampleZh: 我們這次小考都不及格,大家處境相同。

vocab.zh: 直言不諱地責備;把不滿說出來
vocab.example: When he cut in line, she gave him a piece of her mind.
vocab.exampleZh: 當他插隊時,她毫不客氣地把他罵了一頓。

vocab.word: the ball is in your court
vocab.example: I've made my offer; now the ball is in your court.
vocab.exampleZh: 我已經提出條件了,現在就看你怎麼決定。

vocab.example: The builder cut corners, so the house fell apart quickly.
vocab.exampleZh: 建商偷工減料,所以房子很快就壞了。

vocab.example: Before we start, let's make sure everyone is on the same page.
vocab.exampleZh: 開始前,我們先確認每個人的想法一致。

vocab.example: Could you lend a hand with these heavy boxes?
vocab.exampleZh: 你可以幫忙搬這些重箱子嗎?

vocab.example: After failing the class, he decided to turn over a new leaf and study hard.
vocab.exampleZh: 被當之後,他決定改過自新,好好用功。

vocab.example: By the way, did you finish the science report yet?
vocab.exampleZh: 對了，你的自然報告寫完了嗎?

vocab.example: No wonder you're so tired; you stayed up all night studying.
vocab.exampleZh: 難怪你這麼累，你熬夜念了一整晚的書。

vocab.example: My sister and I take turns washing the dishes after dinner.
vocab.exampleZh: 我和姊姊晚餐後輪流洗碗。

vocab.example: She solved the difficult math problem on her own.
vocab.exampleZh: 她靠自己解開了那道困難的數學題。

vocab.example: Your explanation finally makes sense to me now.
vocab.exampleZh: 你的解釋現在對我來說終於說得通了。

vocab.example: Please get in touch with me if you have any questions.
vocab.exampleZh: 如果你有任何問題，請與我聯絡。

vocab.example: We had a lot of fun at the beach last weekend.
vocab.exampleZh: 上週末我們在海邊玩得很開心。

vocab.example: I wanted pizza, but I changed my mind and ordered noodles.
vocab.exampleZh: 我本來想吃披薩，但改變主意點了麵。

vocab.example: If you work hard, your dream will come true one day.
vocab.exampleZh: 只要努力，你的夢想總有一天會實現。

vocab.example: Just do your best, and don't worry about the result.
vocab.exampleZh: 只要盡力就好，不用擔心結果。

vocab.example: Please keep in mind that the library closes at nine.
vocab.exampleZh: 請記住圖書館九點關門。

vocab.example: I was about to leave when the phone rang.
vocab.exampleZh: 我正要離開時電話響了。

vocab.example: "Can you help me?" "Of course I can!"
vocab.exampleZh: 「你能幫我嗎?」「我當然可以!」

vocab.example: She really cares about her friends' feelings.
vocab.exampleZh: 她真的很在乎朋友的感受。

vocab.example: It took me a while to get used to the new school.
vocab.exampleZh: 我花了一段時間才習慣新學校。

vocab.example: It's easy to make friends when you are friendly.
vocab.exampleZh: 當你友善時就很容易交到朋友。

vocab.example: I brought you some cake to cheer you up.
vocab.exampleZh: 我帶了些蛋糕來讓你開心一點。

vocab.example: Try to see the problem from your friend's point of view.
vocab.exampleZh: 試著從你朋友的角度看這個問題。
